Aim for the head, kid. Saves shells.
Reload between waves, not during. Trust me on that one.
If it's bigger than a cat, use the revolver. If it's bigger than a dog, use both.
Roaches die in clusters. Don't waste a slug on one.
Pigeons fake left, dive right. Always.
The flamethrower hates wind. The wind hates the flamethrower. They get along.
Saint Anthony finds lost things. Including, on occasion, you.
Don't shoot the sewer fish in the water. Wait for them to surface.
Switchblade Charm cuts your reload by a hair. Keeps the rhythm.
Tight Choke for the rooftops; Duckbill for the alleys.
Tracer Rounds give the night a color. Use them in the tunnels.
If you hear chittering, it's already in the room.
Mr. Halpern still owes me a sandwich. Don't break the freezer.
The kid who carried the lucky shell — never loaded it. Worked for him.
Reload after a clean kill, not after a missed one. Build the rhythm.
Don't look up at noon. That's not a saying. That's an instruction.
Variety bonus: switch targets between shots. The score knows.
Headshot streaks compound. Five in a row and the multiplier sticks.
When a boss winds up, that's not the time to reload. Don't be cute.
Concrete Saint is heavier than it looks. Carry it anyway.
Sanitation goes home at six. After that, the city's yours and theirs.
If the door at 142 Mott is wax-sealed, walk away. Try again next week.
Geese are smarter than the gulls. Take the geese first.
The Subway Token brings luck. Don't ask whose.
Two-for-one is when one slug catches two heads. Pure profit.
St-Anthony Medal: don't take it off underwater.
If you're out of shells, you're not out of options. Walk slow. Don't run.
The Pigeon-King has been waiting. Don't keep him waiting longer than you have to.
Mid-air kills tip the scoreboard. Don't ground them first.
Eat before you go out. You can't aim on an empty stomach.
